# UC-22.6.27 · Information Access Restriction — SharePoint Anonymous Link Creation Blocked vs Attempted

## Description

Compares anonymous sharing attempts to blocked operations for access restriction effectiveness.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map Operations to your tenant vocabulary; (2) alert on unexpected allows; (3) DLP alignment. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:management`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map Operations to your tenant vocabulary; (2) alert on unexpected allows; (3) DLP alignment.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload=SharePoint earliest=-30d Operation="*AnonymousLink*"
| eval outcome=if(match(Operation,"(?i)create") AND !match(Operation,"(?i)block"), "allowed_or_pending", "blocked_or_removed")
| stats count by outcome, UserPrincipalName
```

Understanding this SPL

**Information Access Restriction — SharePoint Anonymous Link Creation Blocked vs Attempted (A.8.3)** — Compares anonymous sharing attempts to blocked operations for access restriction effectiveness.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:management, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **outcome** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by outcome, UserPrincipalName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart, Table

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload=SharePoint earliest=-30d Operation="*AnonymousLink*"
| eval outcome=if(match(Operation,"(?i)create") AND !match(Operation,"(?i)block"), "allowed_or_pending", "blocked_or_removed")
| stats count by outcome, UserPrincipalName
```

Column chart, Table. Key fields: `UserPrincipalName`, `outcome`.
